positive.prompt: Verify my CUJs still pass against the running app
Do the journeys still work after this refactor?
Which step of the checkout journey broke?

negative.prompt: Interview me and write down our critical user journeys
Write Playwright end-to-end tests for the checkout flow

evals.prompt: In the Sprout repo, run /ux-agent-skills:audit-cuj against the running app with --cuj all. Sprout's journeys are unmodified and working.
evals.expected_output: A contract-valid report at Sprout/.ux/audits/cuj-<timestamp>.md with auditor: cuj and total: 0, whose executive summary leads with the journey and step counts, and whose Appendix enumerates every verified step.
expectations: Writes ONE report per run (not per CUJ) to Sprout/.ux/audits/cuj-<timestamp>.md that validates against the shared report contract, plus an appended index row
A passing journey yields total: 0 with every passing step enumerated in the Appendix and NEVER emitted as a [sev0] finding
The executive summary leads with 'P/N journeys passed, S skipped, M of T steps verified', where N counts the journeys SELECTED rather than the journeys run
frameworks claims only what the run proved: task-completion appears only because the run actually completed the journeys live
Each journey's preconditions were established via a recorded ladder rung, and the Appendix names which rung established each one
Does not edit any of Sprout's application code
All writes are confined to Sprout/.ux/audits/ — .ux/cujs/ is untouched, and `audit_safety.py <sprout>` under the DEFAULT audit profile reports no violation

evals.prompt: Break Sprout's add-task input so submitting does nothing, then run /ux-agent-skills:audit-cuj --cuj all against the running app.
evals.expected_output: A report containing a severity-4 finding naming CUJ-001 and the exact step whose expected outcome never occurred — detection, not narration.
expectations: Emits a [sev4] finding whose Framework Violation names CUJ-001 and the SPECIFIC step that broke, quoting that step's verbatim expect

Does NOT report CUJ-001 as skipped: its precondition ('at least one existing task') is only reachable through the add-task flow it exists to test, so it is self-referential and must be graded from a bare entry_point rather than alibied into a skip
The severity justification names both stages — the failure class, then the clamp against the journey's own criticality
A journey skipped because CUJ-001 broke does NOT open a second finding for the same bug; the blocker's finding carries an 'Also blocks:' line instead
total: 0 does not appear — a verifier that only ever passes is the failure this eval exists to catch
Does not edit or repair Sprout's application code, and does not soften .ux/cujs/ to make the journey pass

evals.prompt: In the Sprout repo with no dev server running, run /ux-agent-skills:audit-cuj --mode static --cuj all.
evals.expected_output: A static report that traces source only, labels every finding 'potential — unverified', emits frameworks: [cuj-contract] alone, and produces no verified pass.
expectations: Every finding is labelled 'potential — unverified' in its Evidence field, with the reason (no running app)
frameworks is [cuj-contract] alone — task-completion is NOT claimed, because the run never completed the journey, and success-criteria is not claimed because persistence was never observed
No journey is reported as a verified pass; steps that could not be observed are listed in the Appendix 'not observed' rather than counted as passes
The mode recorded in frontmatter is the mode that actually ran
